heading: Sexual assault services program
label: 12511 Sexual assault services program
notes: CodificationSection was formerly classified to , The Public Health and Welfare, prior to editorial reclassification and renumbering as this section.section 14043g of Title 42

children.label: A
children.text: adult, youth, and child victims of sexual assault;

children.label: B
children.text: family and household members of such victims; and

children.label: C
children.text: those collaterally affected by the victimization, except for the perpetrator of such victimization; and

children.label: A
children.text: Federal, State, tribal, territorial and local governments, law enforcement agencies, and courts;

children.label: B
children.text: professionals working in legal, social service, and health care settings;

children.label: C
children.text: nonprofit organizations;

children.label: D
children.text: faith-based organizations; and

children.label: E
children.text: other individuals and organizations seeking such assistance.

children.heading: Grants to States and territories
children.label: b Grants to States and territories

children.heading: Grants authorized
children.label: 1 Grants authorized
children.text: The Attorney General shall award grants to States and territories to support the establishment, maintenance, and expansion of rape crisis centers and other nongovernmental or tribal programs and projects to assist individuals who have been victimized by sexual assault, without regard to the age of the individual.

children.heading: Administrative costs
children.label: A Administrative costs
children.text: Not more than 5 percent of the grant funds received by a State or territory governmental agency under this subsection for any fiscal year may be used for administrative costs.

children.heading: Grant funds
children.label: B Grant funds
children.text: Any funds received by a State or territory under this subsection that are not used for administrative costs shall be used to provide grants to rape crisis centers and other nonprofit, nongovernmental organizations or tribal programs and activities for programs and activities within such State or territory that provide direct intervention and related assistance.

children.label: i
children.text: 24-hour hotline services providing crisis intervention services and referral;

children.label: ii
children.text: accompaniment and advocacy through medical, criminal justice, and social support systems, including medical facilities, police, and court proceedings;

children.label: iii
children.text: crisis intervention, short-term individual and group support services, direct payments, and comprehensive service coordination and supervision to assist sexual assault victims and family or household members;

children.label: iv
children.text: information and referral to assist the sexual assault victim and family or household members;

children.label: v
children.text: community-based, culturally specific services and support mechanisms, including outreach activities for underserved communities; and

children.label: vi
children.text: the development and distribution of materials on issues related to the services described in clauses (i) through (v).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Each eligible entity desiring a grant under this subsection shall submit an application to the Attorney General at such time and in such manner as the Attorney General may reasonably require.

children.label: i
children.text: set forth procedures designed to ensure meaningful involvement of the State or territorial sexual assault coalition and representatives from underserved communities in the development of the application and the implementation of the plans;

children.label: ii
children.text: set forth procedures designed to ensure an equitable distribution of grants and grant funds within the State or territory and between urban and rural areas within such State or territory;

children.label: iii
children.text: identify the State or territorial agency that is responsible for the administration of programs and activities; and

children.label: iv
children.text: meet other such requirements as the Attorney General reasonably determines are necessary to carry out the purposes and provisions of this section.

children.heading: Minimum amount
children.label: 4 Minimum amount
children.text: The Attorney General shall allocate to each State (including the District of Columbia and Puerto Rico) not less than 1.50 percent of the total amount appropriated in a fiscal year for grants under this section, except that the United States Virgin Islands, American Samoa, Guam, and the Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana Islands shall each be allocated 0.5 percent of the total appropriations. The remaining funds shall be allotted to each State and each territory in an amount that bears the same ratio to such remaining funds as the population of such State and such territory bears to the population of all the States and the territories.

children.heading: Grants for culturally specific programs addressing sexual assault
children.label: c Grants for culturally specific programs addressing sexual assault

children.heading: Grants authorized
children.label: 1 Grants authorized
children.text: The Attorney General shall award grants to eligible entities to support the establishment, maintenance, and expansion of culturally specific intervention and related assistance for victims of sexual assault.

children.label: A
children.text: be a private nonprofit organization that focuses primarily on culturally specific communities;

children.label: B
children.text: must have documented organizational experience in the area of sexual assault intervention or have entered into a partnership with an organization having such expertise;

children.label: C
children.text: have expertise in the development of community-based, linguistically and culturally specific outreach and intervention services relevant for the specific communities to whom assistance would be provided or have the capacity to link to existing services in the community tailored to the needs of culturally specific populations; and

children.label: D
children.text: have an advisory board or steering committee and staffing which is reflective of the targeted culturally specific community.

children.heading: Award basis
children.label: 3 Award basis
children.text: The Attorney General shall award grants under this section on a competitive basis.

children.heading: Distribution
children.label: 4 Distribution
children.text: The Attorney General shall not use more than 2.5 percent of funds appropriated under this subsection in any year for administration, monitoring, and evaluation of grants made available under this subsection.

children.heading: Term
children.label: 5 Term
children.text: The Attorney General shall make grants under this section for a period of no less than 2 fiscal years.

children.heading: Technical assistance
children.label: 6 Technical assistance
children.text: The Attorney General shall provide technical assistance to recipients of grants under this subsection by entering into a cooperative agreement or contract with a national, nonprofit, nongovernmental organization or organizations whose primary focus and expertise is in addressing sexual assault within culturally specific communities.

children.heading: Reporting
children.label: 7 Reporting
children.text: Each entity receiving a grant under this subsection shall submit a report to the Attorney General that describes the activities carried out with such grant funds.

children.heading: Grants to State, territorial, and tribal sexual assault coalitions
children.label: d Grants to State, territorial, and tribal sexual assault coalitions

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Attorney General shall award grants to State, territorial, and tribal sexual assault coalitions to assist in supporting the establishment, maintenance, and expansion of such coalitions.

children.heading: Minimum amount
children.label: B Minimum amount
children.text: Not less than 10 percent of the total amount appropriated to carry out this section shall be used for grants under subparagraph (A).

children.heading: Eligible applicants
children.label: C Eligible applicants
children.text: Each of the State, territorial, and tribal sexual assault coalitions.

children.label: A
children.text: work with local sexual assault programs and other providers of direct services to encourage appropriate responses to sexual assault within the State, territory, or tribe;

children.label: B
children.text: work with judicial and law enforcement agencies to encourage appropriate responses to sexual assault cases;

children.label: C
children.text: work with courts, child protective services agencies, and children’s advocates to develop appropriate responses to child custody and visitation issues when sexual assault has been determined to be a factor;

children.label: D
children.text: design and conduct public education campaigns;

children.label: E
children.text: plan and monitor the distribution of grants and grant funds to their State, territory, or tribe; or

children.label: F
children.text: collaborate with and inform Federal, State, or local public officials and agencies to develop and implement policies to reduce or eliminate sexual assault.

children.label: A
children.text: not less than 10 percent of the funds shall be available for grants to tribal sexual assault coalitions; and

children.label: B
children.text: the remaining funds shall be available for grants to State and territorial coalitions, and the Attorney General shall allocate an amount equal to ⁄ of the amounts so appropriated to each of those State and territorial coalitions.156

children.heading: Application
children.label: 4 Application
children.text: Each eligible entity desiring a grant under this subsection shall submit an application to the Attorney General at such time, in such manner, and containing such information as the Attorney General determines to be essential to carry out the purposes of this section.

children.heading: First-time applicants
children.label: 5 First-time applicants
children.text: No entity shall be prohibited from submitting an application under this subsection during any fiscal year for which funds are available under this subsection because such entity has not previously applied or received funding under this subsection.

children.heading: Grants authorized
children.label: 1 Grants authorized
children.text: The Attorney General may award grants to Indian tribes, tribal organizations, and nonprofit tribal organizations for the operation of sexual assault programs or projects in Indian tribal lands and Alaska Native villages to support the establishment, maintenance, and expansion of programs and projects to assist those victimized by sexual assault.

children.heading: Administrative costs
children.label: A Administrative costs
children.text: Not more than 5 percent of the grant funds received by an Indian tribe, tribal organization, and nonprofit tribal organization under this subsection for any fiscal year may be used for administrative costs.

children.heading: Grant funds
children.label: B Grant funds
children.text: Any funds received under this subsection that are not used for administrative costs shall be used to provide grants to tribal organizations and nonprofit tribal organizations for programs and activities within Indian country and Alaskan native villages that provide direct intervention and related assistance.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated $100,000,000 to remain available until expended for each of fiscal years 2023 through 2027 to carry out the provisions of this section.

children.label: A
children.text: not more than 2.5 percent shall be used by the Attorney General for evaluation, monitoring, and other administrative costs under this section;

children.label: B
children.text: not more than 8 percent shall be used for the provision of technical assistance to grantees and subgrantees under this section of which not less than 20 percent shall be available for technical assistance to recipients and potential recipients of grants under subsection (c);

children.label: C
children.text: not less than 65 percent shall be used for grants to States and territories under subsection (b);

children.label: D
children.text: not less than 10 percent shall be used for making grants to State, territorial, and tribal sexual assault coalitions under subsection (d);

children.label: E
children.text: not less than 10 percent shall be used for grants to tribes under subsection (e); and

children.label: F
children.text: not less than 10 percent shall be used for grants for culturally specific programs addressing sexual assault under subsection (c).
